This psalm is a lesson. It speaks of the coming of Christ and the day of judgment, when God will call people to account; and the Holy Spirit is the spirit of judgment. Everyone needs to know the right way to worship the Lord, in spirit and in truth. On that great day, our God will come and make those hear his judgment who would not listen to his law. Happy are those who enter into the covenant of grace, by faith in the Redeemer's atoning sacrifice, and show the sincerity of their love through acts of righteousness. When God rejects the services of those who rest in outward actions, he will graciously accept those who seek him correctly. It is only by sacrifice, by Christ, the great Sacrifice, from whom the sacrifices of the law derived their value, that we can be accepted by God. His judgments are true and righteous; even sinners' own consciences will be forced to acknowledge the righteousness of God.

To obey is better than sacrifice, and to love God and your neighbor is better than all burnt offerings. We are warned here not to rest in these actions. And let us beware of resting in any form. God demands the heart, and how can human inventions please him when repentance, faith, and holiness are neglected? In times of distress we must turn to the Lord through earnest prayer. Our troubles, even though we see them coming from God’s hand, must drive us to him, not drive us away from him. We must acknowledge him in all our ways, depend on his wisdom, power, and goodness, and commit ourselves completely to him, so that we may give him glory. Thus we must maintain fellowship with God; meeting him with prayers during trials, and with praises in deliverances. A believing petitioner will not only be graciously answered regarding their request, and therefore have reason for praising God, but will also receive grace to praise him.

Hypocrisy is wickedness, which God will judge. It is too common for those who declare the Lord’s statutes to others to live in disobedience to them themselves. This deception arises from the abuse of God's patience and a deliberate misunderstanding of his character and the intention of his gospel. The sins of sinners will be fully proven against them in the judgment of the great day. The day is coming when God will set their sins in order, sins of childhood and youth, of riper age and old age, to their everlasting shame and terror. Let those who have been forgetful of God, given over to wickedness, or in any way negligent of salvation, consider their urgent danger. The patience of the Lord is very great. It is all the more wonderful because sinners misuse it so badly; but if they do not turn, they will be made to see their error when it is too late. Those who forget God, forget themselves; and things will never go right for them until they consider. Man’s chief end is to glorify God: whoever offers praise glorifies him, and his spiritual sacrifices will be accepted. We must praise God, offer praise as a sacrifice, put it into the hands of the Priest, our Lord Jesus, who is also the altar; we must be fervent in spirit, praising the Lord. Let us thankfully accept God’s mercy, and strive to glorify him in word and deed.
